_comment: Macro-Terrain: Ancient Battlefield Bone Plain. Per scale directive: this terrain IS a being. Player shouldn't initially realize they're standing on bone. Divine Sense -> Perception changes -> 'You realize this mountain range... is one finger.' Prime Directive: the being's corpse is objectively terrain; perception tier determines what the player sees.
name: Ancient Battlefield Bone Plain
scale: 500 li across — the bone-crushed remains of millions of fallen cultivators.
canonical_event: An ancient war killed millions here; the battlefield remains saturated with death.

perception_tiered_appearance.mortal: A plain 500 li across, soil is grey-white, oddly fertile.
perception_tiered_appearance.qi_condensation: The plain radiates death-qi; the soil is clearly not normal earth.
perception_tiered_appearance.foundation_establishment: The soil is crushed bone; the plain is a mass grave.
perception_tiered_appearance.core_formation: This is an ancient battlefield; millions of cultivators fell here; the soil is their bone.
perception_tiered_appearance.nascent_soul: The death-essence still saturates the plain; nether beasts cultivate here.
perception_tiered_appearance.spirit_severing: The battlefield's karmic weight warps reality; cultivating here is dangerous.

perception_mechanic.trigger: When player's perception tier rises, the appearance re-rolls automatically.
perception_mechanic.no_locked_upgrades: A mortal who later reaches Core Formation will see the truth; the terrain does not change, only understanding does.
perception_mechanic.canon_fidelity: This is exactly how Wang Lin perceived Ancient God Tu Si's remains — first as terrain, then as god-bone, then as god-memory.
